display_metrics.scale_narrative: The nearest major galaxy to the Milky Way, containing approximately one trillion stars.
A massive star-forming region visible to the naked eye just south of Orion's Belt.
A diffuse emission nebula famously containing the Pillars of Creation.
A giant interstellar cloud and stellar nursery.
A planetary nebula formed by the glowing shell of ionized gas expelled by a dying star.
The expanding remnant of a historical supernova recorded in 1054 AD.
A temperate Earth-sized exoplanet orbiting a cool red dwarf star.
A rocky, Earth-sized exoplanet orbiting within the habitable zone of an ultracool dwarf star.
The supermassive black hole at the galactic center of the Milky Way.
The most distant human-made object, currently coasting through interstellar space.
The only spacecraft to have visited Uranus and Neptune.
The first spacecraft to explore Pluto and the Kuiper belt.
The first spacecraft to travel through the asteroid belt and visit Jupiter.
The first probe to encounter Saturn.
